definition: No robust nonlinear debt/GDP threshold exists in the data. The Reinhart-Rogoff '90% threshold' was debunked (Herndon-Ash-Pollin 2013). A modest linear negative relationship exists: -0.02 to -0.04pp growth per 1pp debt/GDP (p<0.05), but shrinks ~40% for monetary-sovereign, own-currency issuers. Japan at 250%+ without crisis is a devastating outlier.
evidence: Panel regressions: 20 advanced economies, 1950-2024, no significant threshold detected
Herndon-Ash-Pollin 2014: original R-R errors and selection bias documented
Linear coefficient shrinks ~40% for monetary-sovereign issuers
Japan anomaly: 250%+ debt/GDP, 30+ years without crisis
open_questions: Is the linear drag overstated due to endogeneity?
Does Japan's experience generalize or is it unique?
